meta.question: What is the origin of SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19)?

positions.label: Zoonotic spillover at the Huanan market

datasets.label: Huanan market environmental samples & early-case geolocation data
Early SARS-CoV-2 genomic sequences (lineages A and B)
SARS-CoV-2 genome structure & furin cleavage site

factors.label: Epidemiological proximity of the earliest cases to the market
factors.rationale: Spatial clustering of early cases and positive samples at the market is the load-bearing zoonotic argument.

provenance.quote: the earliest known COVID-19 cases were geographically centered on this market

factors.label: Ascertainment / reporting bias in early Wuhan case data
factors.rationale: Argues the market centering is not merely a surveillance artifact.

provenance.quote: cases with no known link to the market were nonetheless geographically distributed near it
early case data reflects where testing was concentrated, not where infection began

factors.label: Number of introductions (single seeding vs multiple spillovers)
factors.rationale: Two independent introductions are hard to reconcile with a single lab escape.

provenance.quote: two separate introductions of lineages A and B into humans

factors.label: Furin cleavage site as evidence of laboratory engineering
factors.rationale: Reads the genome features as consistent with natural evolution.

provenance.quote: the acquisition of both the polybasic cleavage site and predicted O-linked glycans argues against manipulation
can be explained by natural evolutionary mechanisms
a furin cleavage site previously unseen in other SARS-like coronaviruses

factors.label: Prior probability of a research-related accident in Wuhan
factors.rationale: Documented intent to engineer a furin site raises the lab prior.

provenance.quote: proposed exactly the kind of engineering that would produce a virus like SARS-CoV-2

factors.label: Reliability of Bayesian priors and likelihood estimates
factors.rationale: The whole conclusion is driven by chosen priors/likelihoods over shared evidence.

provenance.quote: combining the likelihoods across independent lines of evidence
70 to 1 in favor of a lab origin

sources.title: Worobey et al. — The Huanan market was the early epicenter of the COVID-19 pandemic

position.quote: the earliest known COVID-19 cases from December 2019 were geographically centered on this market; SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples were spatially associated with vendors selling live mammals

sources.title: Pekar et al. — The molecular epidemiology of multiple zoonotic origins of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: the analyses provide support for two separate introductions of lineages A and B into humans, both circulating in non-human mammals prior to their introduction

sources.title: Andersen, Rambaut, Lipkin, Holmes, Garry — The proximal origin of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: our analyses clearly show that SARS-CoV-2 is not a laboratory construct or a purposefully manipulated virus

sources.title: Crits-Christoph et al. — Genetic tracing of market wildlife and viruses at the epicenter of the pandemic

position.quote: SARS-CoV-2-positive samples were significantly enriched for genetic material of susceptible wildlife species, including raccoon dogs

sources.title: Holmes et al. — The origins of SARS-CoV-2: A critical review

position.quote: there is currently no evidence that SARS-CoV-2 has a laboratory origin

sources.title: Emergence of the Spike Furin Cleavage Site in SARS-CoV-2 — a natural-evolution account

position.quote: the furin cleavage site can be explained by natural evolutionary mechanisms without invoking laboratory manipulation

sources.title: Segreto & Deigin — The genetic structure of SARS-CoV-2 does not rule out a laboratory origin

position.quote: the SARS-CoV-2 chimeric structure and furin cleavage site might be the result of laboratory manipulation

sources.title: Analysis of the DEFUSE proposal (EcoHealth / WIV / DARPA, 2018)

position.quote: the DEFUSE proposal described inserting a furin cleavage site into a SARS-related bat coronavirus

sources.title: Rootclaim (Saar Wilf) — Bayesian analysis of COVID-19 origins (lab leak)

position.quote: our Bayesian analysis concludes a laboratory origin is substantially more likely than a market spillover

sources.title: Weissman — An Inconvenient Probability: Bayesian analysis of COVID origins (lab ~70:1)

position.quote: a likelihood of about 70 to 1 in favor of a lab origin, and probably no less than 28 to 1

sources.title: Peter Miller — The case for zoonosis (COVID origins debate)

position.quote: the epidemiological proximity of the earliest cases to the market outweighs the genetic anomalies

sources.title: WHO-China Joint Study on the origins of SARS-CoV-2

position.quote: introduction through a laboratory incident was assessed to be an extremely unlikely pathway

sources.title: US ODNI — Updated Assessment on COVID-19 Origins

position.quote: the IC remains divided; all agencies assess that both a natural and laboratory-associated origin remain plausible

sources.title: Wade — The origin of COVID: Did people or nature open Pandora's box at Wuhan?

position.quote: the furin cleavage site and the lack of an identified intermediate host point toward a laboratory escape

sources.title: Response to Segreto & Deigin — There is still no evidence of SARS-CoV-2 laboratory origin

position.quote: neither the furin cleavage site nor the genome structure constitutes evidence of laboratory manipulation

sources.title: Scott Alexander — I watched 15 hours of COVID origins arguments so you don't have to

position.quote: both judges decided in favor of Peter; I found the evidence in favor of zoonotic origin to be strong though not insurmountable
